executive_summary: Canada is experiencing a massive ransomware attack wave with over 100 organizations compromised across all sectors in the past 48 hours. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group is the most active threat actor with 18 confirmed victims, followed by Play, Clop, and SafePay groups. Multiple critical infrastructure sectors are affected including healthcare, education, public sector, and financial services. No CCCS advisories were issued during this period despite the unprecedented attack volume.

items.name: Qilin (Agenda)
items.targets: 18 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter, T1003 OS Credential Dumping

items.name: Clop (Cl0p)
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Strategic Objectives, Ideal Welders, Conwest, CMH Heli, Granite Pointe, SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Whiskijack Resorts, Technikel, Modtech, East Plats, Centaur Products
items.ttps: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1048 Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol, T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 10 Canadian organizations including Briway Carriers, Matt and Steve, Ripobec, Groupe Piche, Precision Aluminum, Kenalex, Silverline Group, Teccart, Mactavish Co

items.targets: 7 Canadian organizations including CPK Interior, Excavation Tourigny, Glassers TV, Steelworks Inc, Bison Family Medical, Omega Tool Corp, Reidhurst Nagy, Bais Yaakov

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors within 48 hours. Notable victims span critical infrastructure including public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), and major commercial brands. No CCCS advisories were issued despite the severity and scale of attacks.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (luxury apparel), Active Green + Ross (automotive), Kids & Company (childcare services)
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (confirmed database leak), Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash financial services

report_2w.executive_summary: Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect VPN is under active attack via CVE-2026-0257, a high-severity authentication bypass vulnerability that allows attackers to bypass login using crafted cookies. GitHub has suffered a security incident affecting internal repositories, triggering a Canadian government advisory (AL26-013). Major data breaches include Charter Communications (4.9 million accounts), HDFC Bank investors, and the Colombian fintech Addi. Separately, malicious npm packages are profiling developer environments through dependency confusion attacks.

items.description: Authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect VPN allowing unauthorized access via crafted cookies
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.description: Path traversal vulnerability (historical reference in IOC data)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.name: termite
items.targets: UEI College - allegedly exposed health records
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: kairos
items.targets: Commune De Camiers (France)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Addi (Colombian fintech) - breach occurred March 2026
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1552 (Unsecured Credentials)

items.name: CMDORGANIZATION (Ransomware)
items.targets: Lake Washington School District
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Unknown (npm supply chain attacker)
items.targets: Developer environments via 33 malicious npm packages
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Unknown (Hugging Face supply chain attacker)
items.targets: Developers using Hugging Face platform - MicrosoftSystem64 malware
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 on May 29, 2026, regarding a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories. No Canadian retail or financial institutions were directly named in breach reports during this period. General Canadian content focused on border security, science innovation, and non-cyber topics.
